query: Protein design binder success rates computational AI RFdiffusion ProteinMPNN 2025 2026 state of the art
answer:
Now I have gathered comprehensive information. Let me compile my research findings.

---

# Computational AI Protein Binder Design: Success Rates and State-of-the-Art (2025-2026)

## Executive Summary

The field of computational protein binder design has undergone rapid transformation since 2022, driven by advances in deep learning methods including RFdiffusion, ProteinMPNN, AlphaFold2, and newer tools like BindCraft, AlphaProteo, RFdiffusion3, and Proteina-Complexa. Success rates have improved dramatically from <0.1% with traditional physics-based methods (e.g., Rosetta) to 10-100% with modern AI approaches, though performance varies significantly by target and methodology.

---

## Key Methods and Success Rates

### 1. RFdiffusion (Baker Lab, 2023)

**RFdiffusion** is a diffusion-based generative model for de novo protein structure design that has become a foundational tool in the field.

**Success Rate Data:**
- **In silico success rate (standard hotspot conditioning):** ~0.98% of designs meet quality metrics (pAE_interaction <10, pLDDT >85, ΔΔG <-30)
- **With β-strand interface conditioning:** ~9.2% in silico success rate (5-fold improvement over standard settings)
- **Experimental validation:** Studies report low success rates for biochemical detection applications (2025 bioRxiv study on Strep-Tag II and cytokines showed limited functional binders)
- **TNFR superfamily binders:** 6 of 96 designs (6.25%) bound TNFR1 in initial screen; partial diffusion optimization improved this significantly

**Limitations identified (2025):**
- Low-affinity designs common
- Inconsistent recombinant expression
- Limited success for polar/hydrophilic targets

---

### 2. ProteinMPNN (Baker Lab, 2022)

**ProteinMPNN** is a deep learning-based protein sequence design method that significantly outperforms physics-based Rosetta.

**Key Metrics:**
- **Native sequence recovery:** 52.4% (vs. 32.9% for Rosetta)
- **Experimental expression success:** 73 of 96 designs (76%) expressed solubly
- **Correct oligomeric state:** 50 of 96 designs (52%) achieved target state
- **Computational efficiency:** ~1.2 seconds per 100 residues vs. ~259 seconds for Rosetta
- **AlphaFold success rate:** 6.7% at lower accuracy cutoff; 1.8% at higher accuracy

**Sequence Design Pipeline Efficiency:**
- ProteinMPNN-FR (combined with Rosetta relaxation): ~6.6% AF2 pAE_interaction <10 pass rate
- ~8-fold efficiency improvement over Rosetta-design alone

---

### 3. LigandMPNN (Baker Lab, 2025)

**Extension of ProteinMPNN for small molecules, nucleotides, and metals.**

**Sequence Recovery Rates (near-ligand residues):**
| Context | Rosetta | ProteinMPNN | LigandMPNN |
|---------|---------|-------------|------------|
| Small molecules | 50.4% | 50.4% | **63.3%** |
| Nucleotides | 35.2% | 34.0% | **50.5%** |
| Metals | 36.0% | 40.6% | **77.5%** |

**Experimental validation:** >100 small-molecule and DNA-binding proteins validated with nanomolar affinity; up to 100-fold binding improvement through LigandMPNN redesign.

---

### 4. BindCraft (EPFL/Correia Lab, August 2025)

**AlphaFold2-based hallucination pipeline achieving breakthrough success rates.**

**Published Success Rates:**
- **Overall average:** 46.3% experimental success rate
- **Range across targets:** 10% (HER2) to 100% (SpCas9)

**Target-Specific Results:**
| Target | Success Rate | Best Affinity |
|--------|--------------|---------------|
| SpCas9 | 100% (6/6) | 267 nM |
| CLDN1 | 85.7% (6/7) | nanomolar |
| PD-L1 | 77.8% (7/9) | 615 nM |
| Der f21 | 57.1% (4/7) | 793 nM |
| BBF-14 | 54.5% (6/11) | 20.9 nM |
| Der f7 | 40% (4/10) | 12.8 nM |
| IFNAR2 | 33.3% (3/9) | 260 nM |
| Bet v1 | 28.6% (2/7) | 120 nM |
| CD45 | 25% (4/16) | 14.7 nM |
| PD-1 | 24.5% (13/53) | <1 nM |
| HER2 | 10% (1/10) | functional |

**In Silico Success:**
- 16.8-62.7% of AF2 trajectories show satisfactory confidence
- 0.6-65.9% pass final computational filters

**Structural Accuracy:** Crystal structures validated at 1.7-3.1 Å backbone RMSD

---

### 5. AlphaProteo (Google DeepMind, September 2024)

**Closed-source system achieving state-of-the-art performance.**

**Experimental Success Rates:**
| Target | AlphaProteo | RFdiffusion |
|--------|-------------|-------------|
| BHRF1 | **88%** | — |
| VEGF-A | **33%** | — |
| IL-7RA | **25%** | 17% |
| PD-L1 | **15%** | 13% |
| SC2RBD | **12%** | — |
| TrkA | **9%** | 4% |
| IL-17A | **14%** | 0.02% |
| TNFα | **0%** | — |

**Key Performance Metrics:**
- **3- to 300-fold better binding affinities** than best existing methods
- **5- to 100-fold higher success rates** than previous approaches
- **Up to 700-fold improvement** on IL-17A vs. RFdiffusion
- Binding affinities: **80-960 picomolar** for 4 targets; low-nanomolar for 3 others

---

### 6. β-Strand Targeted RFdiffusion (Baker Lab, January 2026)

**Specialized conditioning for hydrophilic β-sheet targets.**

**In Silico Success Rates:**
- **β-strand interface conditioning:** 9.2%
- **Standard hotspot conditioning:** 0.98%
- **Improvement:** 5-fold higher in silico success

**Experimental Results:**
- Designed binders with **pM to mid-nM affinities**
- Successfully targeted previously intractable targets (ALK-3)
- Affinities achieved: **1.9 nM (α-CTX)**, **101 nM (NRP1)**, **76 pM (KIT)**

---

### 7. RFdiffusion3 (Baker Lab, 2025-2026)

**All-atom diffusion model for diverse biomolecular targets.**

**In Silico Benchmarks:**
- **Protein binders:** RFD3 outperforms RFD1 on 4/5 targets
- **Average unique successful clusters:** 8.2 (RFD3) vs. 1.4 (RFD1)
- **DNA binders:** 8.67% monomeric, 6.67% dimeric pass rate
- **Small molecule binders:** 10-42% depending on ligand

**Experimental Results (limited):**
- DNA binders: 1/5 designs tested successfully (EC50 = 5.89 µM)
- Cysteine hydrolases: 35/190 (18%) showed multi-turnover activity

**Speed:** 10x faster inference than RFdiffusion2

---

### 8. Proteina-Complexa (NVIDIA/ICLR 2026)

**Unified generative + hallucination approach with inference-time optimization.**

**Benchmark Performance (without test-time optimization):**

*Small Molecule Targets:*
| Model | SAM | OQO | FAD | IAI | Time (s) |
|-------|-----|-----|-----|-----|----------|
| RFDiffusion-AllAtom | 2 | 3 | 5 | 8 | 87.4 |
| **Proteina-Complexa** | **10** | **6** | **17** | **19** | **13.5** |

*Protein Targets (unique successes):*
| Model | Self | MPNN-FI | MPNN | Best on # targets |
|-------|------|---------|------|-------------------|
| RFDiffusion | — | — | 3 | 70.8 |
| Protpardelle-1c | — | — | 0 | 8.13 |
| APM | 1 | 0 | 1 | 73.1 |
| **Proteina-Complexa** | **14** | **14** | **14** | **15.6** |

**With Inference-Time Optimization:**
| Configuration | Unique Successes (avg.) | Interface H-bonds (avg.) |
|---------------|-------------------------|--------------------------|
| No reward | 77.0 | 5.27 |
| With f_ipAE | 83.4 | 5.52 |
| With f_H-Bond | 82.4 | 7.15 |
| With both | **86.3** | 6.52 |

**Experimental Validation:** Extensive wet-lab testing against 133 targets; binders achieved nano- to picomolar affinities.

---

## Deep Learning Augmentation Impact (Bennett et al., 2023)

**Using AlphaFold2/RoseTTAFold for filtering:**
- **~10-fold increase** in experimental success rates vs. energy-based methods alone
- **Retrospective analysis:** AF2 pAE_interaction <10 filter significantly enriches for experimental binders
- **ProteinMPNN vs. Rosetta:** Similar experimental success rates but ~175x faster computation

---

## Summary Table: State-of-the-Art Success Rates (2024-2026)

| Method | Type | Experimental Success Rate | Notes |
|--------|------|---------------------------|-------|
| **Traditional Rosetta** | Physics-based | <0.1% | Requires extensive screening |
| **RFdiffusion (standard)** | Generative | <1% in silico | Requires sequence re-design |
| **RFdiffusion (β-targeting)** | Generative | ~9.2% in silico | 5-fold improvement |
| **ProteinMPNN** | Sequence design | 76% expression, 52% correct state | Fast, reliable |
| **BindCraft** | Hallucination | **10-100%** (avg. 46.3%) | Open-source, no screening needed |
| **AlphaProteo** | Generative+Filter | **9-88%** | Closed-source, state-of-the-art |
| **RFdiffusion3** | All-atom diffusion | 10-42% in silico | Versatile targets |
| **Proteina-Complexa** | Unified generative | State-of-the-art on benchmarks | Open-source, test-time scaling |

---

## Key Challenges Identified

1. **TNFα remains intractable** for all methods tested (AlphaProteo: 0%; difficult target)
2. **Polar/hydrophilic targets** show lower success rates
3. **In silico success ≠ experimental binding** (validation gaps remain)
4. **Affinity optimization** often requires additional rounds
5. **Target-dependent variability** in success rates is substantial (10% to 100%)
6. **Functional validation** beyond binding is sparse in most studies

---

## Conclusions

The field has achieved remarkable progress from 2022-2026:
- Success rates improved from <0.1% to 10-100% for well-characterized targets
- BindCraft and AlphaProteo represent current state-of-the-art for experimental success
- RFdiffusion/ProteinMPNN pipeline remains widely used and foundational
- Newer methods (RFdiffusion3, Proteina-Complexa) push toward all-atom and unified approaches
- Test-time compute scaling emerges as important enhancement strategy
- Average affinities in nanomolar range; some picomolar binders achieved without optimization

key_facts: BindCraft (EPFL, August 2025) achieves 10-100% experimental success rates (average 46.3%) across 12 targets, representing state-of-the-art open-source performance
AlphaProteo (Google DeepMind, September 2024) achieved 9-88% experimental success rates and 3-300x better binding affinities than previous methods on 7 targets
Standard RFdiffusion with hotspot conditioning achieves approximately 0.98% in silico success rate; β-strand interface conditioning improves this 5-fold to 9.2%
ProteinMPNN achieves 52.4% native sequence recovery vs 32.9% for Rosetta, with 76% soluble expression and 52% correct oligomeric state experimentally
Deep learning filtering (AlphaFold2/RoseTTAFold) increases experimental binder design success rates approximately 10-fold compared to physics-based methods alone
Proteina-Complexa (NVIDIA, March 2026) sets new state-of-the-art on computational benchmarks by unifying generative and hallucination approaches with test-time optimization
Traditional physics-based methods (Rosetta) have experimental success rates below 0.1%, requiring extensive high-throughput screening
LigandMPNN (March 2025) achieves 63.3% sequence recovery for small molecules vs 50.4% for ProteinMPNN, and 77.5% for metals vs 40.6%
RFdiffusion3 achieves 8.2 unique successful clusters on average vs 1.4 for RFdiffusion1 on protein targets, with 10x faster inference
TNFα remains intractable for all tested methods including AlphaProteo (0% success), representing a persistent challenge in the field
BindCraft-designed binders achieve affinities ranging from sub-nanomolar to low-nanomolar without experimental optimization
AlphaProteo achieved 88% success rate on BHRF1 and 33% on VEGF-A (first successful AI-designed binder for VEGF-A)
β-pairing targeted RFdiffusion produced binders with picomolar to mid-nanomolar affinities including 76 pM for KIT
Success rates are highly target-dependent, ranging from 10% (HER2 with BindCraft) to 100% (SpCas9 with BindCraft)
Inference-time optimization (beam search, MCTS) can increase unique successes from 77 to 86.3 average using Proteina-Complexa
